neighborhoods.name: Vieux Nice (Old Town)
neighborhoods.description: Baroque old quarter with the famous Cours Saleya flower market, narrow streets, and vibrant Mediterranean life
neighborhoods.character: Baroque historic, market culture, Mediterranean vibrant
neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Nice. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Cimiez
neighborhoods.description: Elegant hillside quarter with Roman ruins, Matisse Museum, belle epoque hotels, and panoramic views
neighborhoods.character: Elegant hillside, museums, belle epoque grandeur

housing.predominantType: Spacious apartments in belle epoque buildings and villas

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Nice's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Saint-Roch / Pasteur
neighborhoods.description: Residential areas north of the center with lower rents and good tram connections

housing.predominantType: Apartments in modern and renovated buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Promenade des Anglais / Carre d'Or
neighborhoods.description: Iconic seafront promenade area with luxury hotels, international residents, and Mediterranean glamour

housing.predominantType: Luxury apartments and belle epoque residences

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
